# Zscaler Identity Protection (ITDR) | Zscaler

**Source:** https://www.zscaler.com/products-and-solutions/zscaler-itdr
**Captured:** 2026-04-25 via WebFetch.

---

## Value Proposition

"Stop Identity-Based Attacks with Continuous Identity Protection" through real-time threat detection and response capabilities that mitigate risks from identity exploitation, credential misuse, and MFA bypass attacks.

## Key Capabilities

- **Visibility & Assessment**
  - Unified risk scoring to quantify and track identity posture
  - Real-time visibility into misconfigurations, risky permissions, and exposed credentials
  - Identity security assessments with intuitive risk scoring

- **Detection & Response**
  - Real-time identity threat detection and response (ITDR)
  - Alerts on configuration and permission changes introducing new risks
  - Detection of attacks including DCSync, DCShadow, kerberoasting, and LDAP enumeration
  - MITRE ATT&CK mapping for blind spot visibility

- **Remediation & Guidance**
  - Step-by-step remediation guidance including video tutorials, scripts, and commands
  - Identification of risky configurations (shared passwords, stale passwords, unconstrained delegation)
  - Endpoint credential exposure detection and cleanup capabilities
  - Password analysis for compromised/weak credentials

- **Threat Containment**
  - Native integration with Zscaler Private Access for real-time threat containment
  - SIEM and EDR integrations
  - SOC workflow integration for automated response

## Deployment & Integration

**Built-in Platform:**
- Lightweight endpoint agent integrated into Zscaler Client Connector (ZCC)
- Simple, low-touch setup achievable "in just minutes"

**Integration Points:**
- Zscaler Private Access (ZPA)
- SIEM platforms
- Leading EDR solutions

## Detection Capabilities

Detects and prevents:
- Kerberoasting attacks
- DCSync and DCShadow attacks
- LDAP enumeration attacks
- Privilege escalation abuse
- Lateral movement exploitation
- Credential theft and misuse

## Key Statistics Referenced

- 50% of organizations fall victim to Active Directory attacks (EMA)
- 75% of 2023 access attacks were malware-free (CrowdStrike)
- 91% consider identity security top 5 priority (BeyondTrust)
